spec_typo_corrected.field: §1 Tier-2 list, arXiv ID for 'Symmetries in PIV and Okamoto polynomials'
spec_typo_corrected.spec_value_actually_is: Schlichenmaier 'Sugawara Construction for Higher Genus Riemann Surfaces' (Rept.Math.Phys. 43, 323-339, 1999)
spec_typo_corrected.substantive_impact: zero (the NY PIV paper is A-type, not the PIII bridge; would not have been acquired regardless)

noumi_yamada_2004_no_PIII_chapter.finding: AMS digital TOC for MMONO/223 enumerates 8 chapters (Bäcklund / symmetric form / τ-functions / τ-on-lattice / Jacobi-Trudi / determinants / Gauss-decomposition / Lax). NO chapter is dedicated to Painlevé III.
noumi_yamada_2004_no_PIII_chapter.why_unexpected: Spec §0 stated 'The book contains a dedicated chapter on PIII / PIII' with explicit generators in *both* conventions and typically an explicit bridge proposition / corollary.' This characterization appears to be incorrect or based on a different memory of the book's contents.
noumi_yamada_2004_no_PIII_chapter.implication: Predicted-absence of W(B_2) ↔ W((2A_1)^(1)) homomorphism in NY 2004 strengthens substantially. ILL is still recommended (operator-side) for definitive confirmation, but probability of homomorphism being present is LOW (estimated <20%).

kny_2017_does_not_contain_W_B2.finding: Kajiwara-Noumi-Yamada 2017 (arXiv:1509.08186, J.Phys.A 50, 073001), the canonical 168-page review, contains 0 occurrences of 'B_2' / 'B(2)' / 'B_{2}' as a Lie-group symbol. Only one stray 'B 2' grep hit is the journal name 'Phys. Lett. B 236' in citation [7].
kny_2017_does_not_contain_W_B2.why_unexpected: If the W(B_2) → W((2A_1)^(1)) bridge were standard folklore in the NY-circle PIII community, one would expect KNY 2017 (the most comprehensive post-Sakai review by the same author group) to mention it at least in passing. Its complete absence suggests the bridge is *not* part of the working vocabulary of post-Sakai PIII research; the Sakai surface-classification framework has effectively replaced the Okamoto W(B_2) framing without retracing the translation.
kny_2017_does_not_contain_W_B2.implication_for_M6_phase_B5: M6 Phase B.5 W cross-walk anchor cannot be sourced from NY-family OA literature. Either (a) the bridge exists in pre-Sakai Okamoto-circle literature that we have not yet probed (Umemura, Watanabe — separate Researcher prompt), or (b) it is folklore-Lie-theory that has never been written out as a theorem in the PIII context, in which case SIARC can derive it directly from Cartan matrix data.

noumi_yamada_2004_amazon_subtitle_predic.finding: AMS bookstore abstract verbatim: 'The present book is devoted to the symmetry of Painlevé equations (especially those of types II and IV).'
noumi_yamada_2004_amazon_subtitle_predic.implication: Author-stated focus is W(\tilde A_1) (PII) and W(\tilde A_2) (PIV), both simply-laced A-type. PIII (rank-2 non-simply-laced B_2 / extended (2A_1)) is structurally orthogonal to these examples — confirms TOC-based prediction that PIII is at most peripheral in NY 2004.
